title: Inbox item promoted to processed/ despite GIT_PUSH_REJECTED — promotion must gate on the ship commit LANDING (ancestor of main or pushed), never on audit verdict alone
summary: Batch b63fyf1ai, skill-overlays-bridge-layer lane (cycle 598): ship push rejected (origin diverged mid-batch), orchestrator routed recovery to precondition-reaudit, the cycle ended FinalVerdict PASS and inbox-mover PROMOTED the item to processed/cycle-598/ — but the work never landed on ANY ref (confirmed via git log --all -S over policy/: nothing). A directive was silently dropped from the queue while reported done. Operator hand-requeued it this time.
root_cause: Promotion criterion is the cycle verdict, not delivery: inbox-mover promotes on cycle PASS, and an audit PASS can precede a ship whose push/merge ultimately fails inside recovery paths. Verdict is not delivery.
fix: Gate promotion on delivery evidence: inbox-mover promotes ONLY when the cycle's ship commit (read from ship-binding.json in the run dir) is an ancestor of local main or present on origin — ancestry check via git merge-base --is-ancestor. Otherwise the item RETURNS to inbox with a retry note (mirror the failed-cycle release path). Recovery flows that end without landing (reaudit loops, push-reject exhaustion) must hit the release path, never promotion.
acceptance: RED: promotion refused when the ship commit is absent from main ancestry (fixture run dir with PASS verdict + unlanded SHA); twin: landed SHA promotes
RED: reaudit-recovery terminal without landing releases the item back with a note
connects_to: ship-repair-merge-diverged-origin / evolve sync-main (the divergence root cause)
